intro_text: p_l wanders past an old badger sett in the roots of a hazelnut tree, only to freeze. There's new scent here, acrid and sour.
decline_text: Surely it must be nothing... Uneasily, the patrol continues on.

success_outcomes.text: Calling the rest of the patrol over, the cats discuss their next move. If a badger has moved in, they need to confirm it, but how? p_l comes up with a plan: stuffing bushes into the mouth of the sett. The cats will be able to come back tomorrow to see if anything has shoved them out of the way.

log.cats_from: cat_from approved of cat_to's plan to check whether a new badger had moved into an old sett.

success_outcomes.text: p_l organizes the patrol, and together they lift a long, skinny branch from the forest floor, shoving the end into the sett. With half the branch's length to go, there's an outraged explosion of fizzing from the depths of the burrow. The patrol retreats to report back to camp. With any luck, it's been injured, but no one wants to check.

log.cats_from: cat_from approved of cat_to's plan to push a branch into a badger sett to check its occupancy.

success_outcomes.text: s_c decides there has to be a smarter way of figuring out if a badger has moved in, one that doesn't involve sticking anyone's head in front of a predator. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/s_c/lead/leads} the patrol on a search of the clearing around the sett until they come upon some fresh badger scat — stinky, horrible, and perfect proof of the new occupant of the sett.

When the badger pokes its head above the ground at dusk, they're prepared, and in unison everyone turns for home. The cats fade into the underbrush before the predator has even blinked the sleep out of its eyes.

intro_text: Padding through the forest at night on a dare, the apprentices gasp as light streaks across the sky. A StarClan cat has fallen!
